com.tonic.jaloc.impl.arrays

Class PBoolArray

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeArray<W>
      • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractPrimitiveArray<PBoolWriter>
        • com.tonic.jaloc.impl.arrays.PBoolArray
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class PBoolArray
extends AbstractPrimitiveArray<PBoolWriter>
    A fixed-length native bool array, bit-packed eight per byte.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PBoolArray(long length)
      Allocates length elements on the system allocator, zeroed.
      PBoolArray(NativeAllocator allocator, long length)
      Allocates length elements on the given allocator, zeroed.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void clearRange(long fromIndex, long toIndex)
      Zeroes fromIndex inclusive to toIndex exclusive.
      boolean get(long index)
      Reads the element at index.
      boolean getUnchecked(long index)
      Reads the element at index with no liveness or bounds check; the caller must have proven both.
      void set(long index, boolean value)
      Writes the element at index.
      void setUnchecked(long index, boolean value)
      Writes the element at index with no liveness or bounds check; the caller must have proven both.
      PBoolWriter writer() 

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractPrimitiveArray

        elementSize

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeArray

        byteSize, clear, close, isEmpty, isOpen, length, writer

    • Constructor Detail

      • PBoolArray

        public PBoolArray(long length)
        Allocates length elements on the system allocator, zeroed.
        Parameters:
        length - the element count
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if length is negative

      • PBoolArray

        public PBoolArray(NativeAllocator allocator,
                  long length)
        Allocates length elements on the given allocator, zeroed.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        length - the element count
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if length is negative

      • get

        public boolean get(long index)
        Reads the element at index.
        Parameters:
        index - the element index
        Returns:
        the element
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of range
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • set

        public void set(long index,
                boolean value)
        Writes the element at index.
        Parameters:
        index - the element index
        value - the value to store
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of range
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • getUnchecked

        public boolean getUnchecked(long index)
        Reads the element at index with no liveness or bounds check; the caller must have proven both.
        Parameters:
        index - the element index
        Returns:
        the element

      • setUnchecked

        public void setUnchecked(long index,
                         boolean value)
        Writes the element at index with no liveness or bounds check; the caller must have proven both.
        Parameters:
        index - the element index
        value - the value to store

      • writer

        public PBoolWriter writer()
        Specified by:
        writer in class AbstractNativeArray<PBoolWriter>
        Returns:
        a fresh writer at position zero

      • clearRange

        public void clearRange(long fromIndex,
                       long toIndex)
        Description copied from class: AbstractNativeArray
        Zeroes fromIndex inclusive to toIndex exclusive.

        toIndex - the range end, exclusive
